translations.literal: One of his retainers offended Xin. Xin imprisoned him and wanted to kill him.
translations.idiomatic: One of Han Xin's attendants offended him; Han Xin jailed him and planned to execute him.

sentences.zh: 舍人弟上書變告信欲反狀於呂后。

translations.literal: The retainer's younger brother submitted a memorial, reporting to Empress Lu the condition that Xin wanted to rebel.
translations.idiomatic: The attendant's younger brother then submitted a memorial to Empress Lu, exposing Han Xin's rebellion plot.

sentences.zh: 呂后欲召，恐其黨不亂，乃與蕭相國謀，詐令人從帝所來，稱豨已死，群臣皆賀。

translations.literal: Empress Lu wanted to summon Xin, but feared his faction would create disorder. So she and Chancellor Xiao plotted, and falsely had someone come from the emperor's camp saying Chen Xi had already died and all ministers were congratulating.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lu wanted Han Xin brought in but feared his followers would revolt. She and Chancellor Xiao devised a ruse: they sent a messenger pretending to come from the emperor, announcing Chen Xi was dead and that all ministers were offering congratulations.

sentences.zh: 相國紿信曰：「雖病，強入賀。」

translations.literal: The chancellor deceived Xin, saying: Though ill, force yourself to enter and offer congratulations.
translations.idiomatic: Xiao He deceived Han Xin, saying, Even if you are ill, you must come in and offer congratulations.

sentences.zh: 信入，呂后使武士縛信，斬之長樂鍾室。

translations.literal: Xin entered. Empress Lu had armed guards bind Xin and behead him in the Bell Chamber of Changle.
translations.idiomatic: Han Xin entered, and Empress Lu had guards seize him and execute him in Changle Palace's Bell Chamber.

sentences.zh: 信方斬，曰：「吾不用蒯通計，反為女子所詐，豈非天哉！」

translations.literal: As Xin was being beheaded, he said: I did not use Kuai Tong's plan; instead I was deceived by a woman. Is this not Heaven!
translations.idiomatic: As he was about to die, Han Xin said, I refused Kuai Tong's plan, only to be tricked by a woman. Is this not fate?

sentences.zh: 遂夷信三族。

translations.literal: Then they exterminated Xin's three clans.
translations.idiomatic: His three clans were then exterminated.

sentences.zh: 高祖已破豨歸，至，聞信死，且喜且哀之，問曰：「信死亦何言？」

translations.literal: After Gaozu had already broken Chen Xi and returned, on arrival he heard Xin was dead. He was both pleased and grieved for him, and asked: What did Xin say when dying?
translations.idiomatic: After Gaozu crushed Chen Xi and returned, he heard Han Xin was dead. He was both relieved and saddened, and asked what Han Xin had said at his death.

sentences.zh: 呂后道其語。

translations.literal: Empress Lu told him his words.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lu repeated Han Xin's words.

sentences.zh: 高祖曰：「此齊辯士蒯通也。」

translations.literal: Gaozu said: This is that Qi persuader Kuai Tong.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu said, This concerns Kuai Tong, that persuader from Qi.

sentences.zh: 召欲亨之。

translations.literal: He summoned him, wishing to boil him.
translations.idiomatic: He summoned Kuai Tong and intended to have him boiled alive.

sentences.zh: 通至自說，釋弗誅。

translations.literal: When Tong arrived and explained himself, he was released and not executed.
translations.idiomatic: Kuai Tong came and defended himself, and Gaozu spared him.

sentences.zh: 語在通傳。

translations.literal: The account is in Tong's biography.
translations.idiomatic: The details are in Kuai Tong's biography.

sentences.zh: 彭越字仲，昌邑人也。

translations.literal: Peng Yue, style name Zhong, was a man of Changyi.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue, courtesy name Zhong, was from Changyi.

sentences.zh: 常漁鉅野澤中，為盜。

translations.literal: He often fished in the marshes of Juye and was a bandit.
translations.idiomatic: He often fished in the Juye marshes and lived as an outlaw.

sentences.zh: 陳勝起，或謂越曰：「豪桀相立畔秦，仲可效之。」

translations.literal: When Chen Sheng rose, someone said to Yue: The heroes are each establishing themselves and rebelling against Qin; Zhong, you can emulate this.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Sheng rose in rebellion, someone told Peng Yue, Great men are rising against Qin everywhere. You should do the same.

sentences.zh: 越曰：「兩龍方鬥，且待之。」

translations.literal: Yue said: Two dragons are just now fighting. For now, wait for them.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue said, Two dragons are fighting. Better to wait.

sentences.zh: 居歲餘，澤間少年相聚百餘人，往從越，「請仲為長」，越謝不願也。

translations.literal: After more than a year, over a hundred young men of the marsh gathered and went to follow Yue, asking Zhong to be chief. Yue declined and was unwilling.
translations.idiomatic: More than a year later, over a hundred young men in the marsh came to follow him and asked him to lead them, but he declined.

sentences.zh: 少年強請，乃許。

translations.literal: The young men forcefully asked, and only then he agreed.
translations.idiomatic: They pressed him strongly, and he finally agreed.

sentences.zh: 與期旦日日出時，後會者斬。

translations.literal: He made an appointment: at dawn, at sunrise time. Those who arrive late to the meeting will be beheaded.
translations.idiomatic: He set a time for mustering at sunrise the next day and announced that latecomers would be executed.

sentences.zh: 旦日日出，十餘人後，後者至日中。

translations.literal: At dawn at sunrise, more than ten men were late. The latest arrived at noon.
translations.idiomatic: At sunrise more than ten men were late, and the last did not arrive until noon.

sentences.zh: 於是越謝曰：「臣老，諸君強以為長。

translations.literal: Then Yue addressed them, saying: I am old, and you gentlemen forced me to be your leader.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue then addressed them: I am an old man, and you have forced me to be your leader.

sentences.zh: 今期而多後，不可盡誅，誅最後者一人。」

translations.literal: Now the appointed time had come and many were late. One could not execute all, so execute only the latest one.
translations.idiomatic: The appointed time came, and too many were late to kill them all. So kill only the last to arrive.

sentences.zh: 令校長斬之。

translations.literal: He ordered the squad leader to behead him.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the squad chief to behead the man.

sentences.zh: 皆笑曰：「

translations.literal: All laughed and said:
translations.idiomatic: Everyone laughed and said:

sentences.zh: 何至是！

translations.literal: How could it come to this!
translations.idiomatic: Does it have to go this far?

sentences.zh: 請後不敢。」

translations.literal: We ask pardon; later we will not dare.
translations.idiomatic: Forgive us. We will not dare be late again.

sentences.zh: 於是越乃引一人斬之，設壇祭，令徒屬。

translations.literal: Then Yue himself led out one man and beheaded him, set up an altar and sacrifice, and gave orders to his followers.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue personally had one man executed, then set up an altar and made offerings before issuing orders.

sentences.zh: 徒屬皆驚，畏越，不敢仰視。

translations.literal: All his followers were shocked, feared Yue, and did not dare raise their eyes.
translations.idiomatic: His followers were terrified and did not dare look up.

sentences.zh: 乃行略地，收諸侯散卒，得千餘人。

translations.literal: Then he went to seize territory and gathered the scattered troops of the lords, obtaining more than a thousand men.
translations.idiomatic: He then began seizing territory and collecting scattered troops, building a force of over a thousand.

sentences.zh: 沛公之從碭北擊昌邑，越助之。

translations.literal: When the Duke of Pei moved north from Dang to attack Changyi, Yue assisted him.
translations.idiomatic: When the Duke of Pei attacked Changyi from north of Dang, Peng Yue assisted him.

sentences.zh: 昌邑未下，沛公引兵西。

translations.literal: Changyi had not yet fallen, and the Duke of Pei led troops west.
translations.idiomatic: Before Changyi fell, the Duke of Pei led his army westward.

sentences.zh: 越亦將其眾居鉅野澤中，收魏敗散卒。

translations.literal: Yue also led his men to remain in the Juye marshes and gathered the scattered defeated soldiers of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue returned to the Juye marshes and gathered scattered Wei survivors.

sentences.zh: 項籍入關，王諸侯，還歸，越眾萬餘人無所屬。

translations.literal: When Xiang Ji entered the passes, enfeoffed the lords, and returned, Yue's force of over ten thousand had no affiliation.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Ji entered the passes and enfeoffed the lords, Peng Yue's force of over ten thousand remained unattached.

sentences.zh: 齊王田榮叛項王，漢乃使人賜越將軍印，使下濟陰以擊楚。

translations.literal: When King Tian Rong of Qi rebelled against King Xiang, Han then sent someone to grant Yue the general's seal and had him descend into Jiyin to attack Chu.
translations.idiomatic: When Tian Rong of Qi rebelled, Han granted Peng Yue a general's seal and sent him into Jiyin to strike Chu.

sentences.zh: 楚令蕭公角將兵擊越，越大破楚軍。

translations.literal: Chu ordered Xiao Gongjiao to lead troops against Yue, and Yue greatly defeated the Chu army.
translations.idiomatic: Chu sent Xiao Gongjiao against him, but Peng Yue crushed the Chu force.

sentences.zh: 漢二年春，與魏豹及諸侯東擊楚，越將其兵三萬餘人，歸漢外黃。

translations.literal: In spring of Han's second year, together with Wei Bao and the lords they attacked Chu in the east. Yue led over thirty thousand troops and returned to Han at Waihuang.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of Han year two, he joined Wei Bao and other lords in an eastern campaign against Chu, leading over thirty thousand men back to Han at Waihuang.

sentences.zh: 漢王曰：「彭將軍收魏地，得十餘城，欲急立魏後。

translations.literal: The King of Han said: General Peng has recovered Wei lands and gained over ten cities. I wish to urgently establish a Wei successor.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han said, General Peng has recovered Wei territory and taken more than ten cities. We should quickly establish a Wei successor.

sentences.zh: 今西魏王豹，魏咎從弟，真魏也。」

translations.literal: Now Western Wei king Bao, younger cousin of Wei Jiu, is true Wei lineage.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Bao, cousin of Wei Jiu, is legitimate Wei stock.

sentences.zh: 乃拜越為魏相國，擅將兵，略定梁地。

translations.literal: So he appointed Yue as Chancellor of Wei, giving him independent command of troops, and he roughly pacified Liang territory.
translations.idiomatic: So he made Peng Yue chancellor of Wei with independent command, and Peng Yue pacified much of Liang.

sentences.zh: 漢王之敗彭城解而西也，越皆亡其所下城，獨將其兵北居河上。

translations.literal: When the King of Han was defeated at Pengcheng and withdrew west, Yue lost all the cities he had taken and alone led his troops north to the river.
translations.idiomatic: After Han's defeat at Pengcheng, Peng Yue lost all his gains and withdrew north to the river with only his own troops.

sentences.zh: 漢三年，越常往來為漢游兵擊楚，絕其糧於梁地。

translations.literal: In Han year three, Yue often came and went as Han's mobile troops attacking Chu, cutting Chu grain in Liang territory.
translations.idiomatic: In Han year three, Peng Yue repeatedly raided for Han in Liang, cutting Chu's supply lines.

sentences.zh: 項王與漢王相距滎陽，越攻下睢陽、外黃十七城。

translations.literal: While King Xiang and King Han faced off at Xingyang, Yue attacked and took Suiyang, Waihuang, and seventeen cities.
translations.idiomatic: While Xiang Yu and Liu Bang confronted each other at Xingyang, Peng Yue captured Suiyang, Waihuang, and seventeen other cities.

sentences.zh: 項王聞之，乃使曹咎守成皋，自東收越所下城邑，皆復為楚。

translations.literal: When King Xiang heard this, he made Cao Jiu guard Chenggao, and from the east personally recovered the cities Yue had taken, all again becoming Chu.
translations.idiomatic: On hearing this, Xiang Yu left Cao Jiu to hold Chenggao and personally moved east to retake Peng Yue's conquests, restoring them to Chu.

sentences.zh: 越將其兵北走穀城。

translations.literal: Yue led his troops north and fled to Gucheng.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue withdrew north to Gucheng.

sentences.zh: 項王南走陽夏，越復下昌邑旁二十餘城，得粟十餘萬斛，以給漢食。

translations.literal: When King Xiang went south to Yangxia, Yue again took more than twenty cities around Changyi, obtaining over one hundred thousand hu of grain to supply Han food.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Yu moved south to Yangxia, Peng Yue took over twenty more cities around Changyi and captured more than a hundred thousand hu of grain for Han.

sentences.zh: 漢王敗，使使召越并力擊楚，越曰：「魏地初定，尚畏楚，未可去。」

translations.literal: After the King of Han was defeated, he sent envoys summoning Yue to combine strength against Chu. Yue said: Wei lands are only newly settled and still fear Chu; I cannot yet leave.
translations.idiomatic: After a Han defeat, Liu Bang sent for Peng Yue to join forces against Chu. Peng Yue replied that Wei territory was newly pacified and still fearful of Chu, so he could not leave.

sentences.zh: 漢王追楚，為項籍所敗固陵。

translations.literal: The King of Han pursued Chu and was defeated by Xiang Ji at Guling.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Bang pursued Chu but was defeated by Xiang Ji at Guling.

sentences.zh: 乃謂留侯曰：「諸侯兵不從，為之奈何？」

translations.literal: Then he said to the Marquis of Liu: The lords' troops do not follow. What should be done?
translations.idiomatic: He then asked Zhang Liang, The lords' armies are not responding. What should we do?

sentences.zh: 留侯曰：「彭越本定梁地，功多，始君王以魏豹故，拜越為相國。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Liu said: Peng Yue originally pacified Liang territory and has much merit. At first, because of Wei Bao, Your Majesty made Yue chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Liang replied, Peng Yue first secured Liang and has great merit. Because of Wei Bao, you made him only chancellor.

sentences.zh: 今豹死亡後，且越亦欲王，而君王不蚤定。

translations.literal: Now after Bao's death, moreover Yue also wishes to be king, and Your Majesty has not settled this early.
translations.idiomatic: Now that Wei Bao is dead, Peng Yue wants kingship, and Your Majesty has not yet settled the matter.

sentences.zh: 今取睢陽以北至穀城，皆許以王彭越。」

translations.literal: Now take the lands from north of Suiyang to Gucheng and promise all to make Peng Yue king.
translations.idiomatic: Grant him all territory from north of Suiyang to Gucheng and promise him kingship.

sentences.zh: 又言所以許韓信。

translations.literal: He also stated what should be promised to Han Xin.
translations.idiomatic: He also explained what should be promised to Han Xin.

sentences.zh: 語在高紀。

translations.literal: The account is in the Annals of Gaozu.
translations.idiomatic: The details are in the Annals of Gaozu.

sentences.zh: 於是漢王發使使越，如留侯策。

translations.literal: So the King of Han sent an envoy to Yue, according to the Marquis of Liu's plan.
translations.idiomatic: So Liu Bang sent envoys to Peng Yue, following Zhang Liang's strategy.

sentences.zh: 使者至，越乃引兵會垓下。

translations.literal: When the envoy arrived, Yue then led troops to join at Gaixia.
translations.idiomatic: When the envoy arrived, Peng Yue marched to Gaixia.

sentences.zh: 項籍死，立越為梁王，都定陶。

translations.literal: After Xiang Ji died, he established Yue as King of Liang, with capital at Dingtao.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Ji's death, Peng Yue was made king of Liang, with his capital at Dingtao.

sentences.zh: 六年，朝陳。

translations.literal: In year six, he came to court at Chen.
translations.idiomatic: In year six, he came to court at Chen.

sentences.zh: 九年，十年，皆來朝長安。

translations.literal: In year nine and year ten, he both times came to court at Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: In years nine and ten, he again came to court at Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 陳豨反代地，高帝自往擊之，至邯鄲，徵兵梁。

translations.literal: When Chen Xi rebelled in Dai territory, Emperor Gao personally went to attack him. Upon reaching Handan, he called for troops from Liang.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Xi rebelled in Dai, Emperor Gao marched personally. At Handan he requisitioned troops from Liang.

sentences.zh: 梁王稱病，使使將兵詣邯鄲。

translations.literal: The King of Liang claimed illness and sent an envoy to lead troops to Handan.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Liang claimed illness and sent troops under an envoy instead.

sentences.zh: 高帝怒，使人讓梁王。

translations.literal: Emperor Gao was enraged and sent someone to reprimand the King of Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gao grew angry and sent a formal rebuke.

sentences.zh: 梁王恐，欲自往謝。

translations.literal: The King of Liang was afraid and wished to go personally to apologize.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Liang grew frightened and wanted to go apologize in person.

sentences.zh: 其將扈輒曰：「王始不往，見讓而往，往即為禽，不如遂發兵反。」

translations.literal: His general Hu Zhe said: At first you did not go. If, after being reproached, you now go, you will be captured on arrival. Better to raise troops and rebel.
translations.idiomatic: His general Hu Zhe said, You refused at first. If you go now after a rebuke, you will be seized. Better to rebel.

sentences.zh: 梁王不聽，稱病。

translations.literal: The King of Liang did not listen and again claimed illness.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Liang did not listen and again claimed illness.

sentences.zh: 梁太僕有罪，亡走漢，告梁王與扈輒謀反。

translations.literal: Liang's Grand Coachman had a crime and fled to Han, reporting that the King of Liang and Hu Zhe planned rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Liang's Grand Coachman, guilty of an offense, fled to Han and accused the king and Hu Zhe of plotting revolt.

sentences.zh: 於是上使使掩捕梁王，囚之雒陽。

translations.literal: Therefore the emperor sent envoys to suddenly arrest the King of Liang and imprison him in Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then sent agents to seize the king of Liang by surprise and imprison him at Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 有司治反形已具，請論如法。

translations.literal: The responsible officials investigated, and the signs of rebellion were complete; they requested sentencing according to law.
translations.idiomatic: Officials completed the case and found full evidence of rebellion, requesting sentence under the law.

sentences.zh: 上赦以為庶人，徙蜀青衣。

translations.literal: The emperor pardoned him and made him a commoner, exiling him to Qingyi in Shu.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor spared him, reduced him to commoner status, and exiled him to Qingyi in Shu.

sentences.zh: 西至鄭，逢呂后從長安東，欲之雒陽，道見越。

translations.literal: Traveling west to Zheng, he met Empress Lu traveling east from Chang'an, wishing to go to Luoyang, and encountered Yue on the road.
translations.idiomatic: On the way west, when he reached Zheng, he met Empress Lu traveling east from Chang'an toward Luoyang, and she encountered Peng Yue on the road.

sentences.zh: 越為呂后泣涕，自言亡罪，願處故昌邑。

translations.literal: Yue wept before Empress Lu and personally said he had no crime, wishing to reside in old Changyi.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue wept before Empress Lu, saying he was innocent and begging to be allowed to live at old Changyi.

sentences.zh: 呂后許諾，詔與俱東。

translations.literal: Empress Lu promised and issued order that he travel east with her.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lu agreed and ordered him to travel east with her.

sentences.zh: 至雒陽，呂后言上曰：「彭越壯士也，今徙之蜀，此自遺患，不如遂誅之。

translations.literal: When they reached Luoyang, Empress Lu said to the emperor: Peng Yue is a strong man. Now exiling him to Shu leaves trouble ourselves. Better to execute him.
translations.idiomatic: At Luoyang, Empress Lu told the emperor, Peng Yue is a formidable man. Exiling him to Shu leaves a future threat; better to execute him.

sentences.zh: 妾謹與俱來。」

translations.literal: I have carefully brought him here together.
translations.idiomatic: I have already brought him back with me.

sentences.zh: 於是呂后令其舍人告越復謀反。

translations.literal: Then Empress Lu ordered one of her attendants to accuse Yue again of plotting rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lu then had an attendant file a fresh accusation of rebellion against Peng Yue.

sentences.zh: 廷尉奏請，遂夷越宗族。

translations.literal: The Commandant of Justice submitted a memorial requesting sentence, and so they exterminated Yue's clan.
translations.idiomatic: The chief justice requested judgment, and Peng Yue's entire clan was exterminated.

sentences.zh: 黥布，六人也，姓英氏。

translations.literal: Qing Bu was a man of Liu and had the Ying surname.
translations.idiomatic: Qing Bu was a man of Liu and bore the surname Ying.

sentences.zh: 少時客相之，當刑而王。

translations.literal: When young, a physiognomist assessed him: he would receive punishment and then be king.
translations.idiomatic: When he was young, a physiognomist predicted that he would first suffer penal punishment and later become king.

sentences.zh: 及壯，坐法黥，布欣然笑曰：「人相我當刑而王，幾是乎？」

translations.literal: When he reached adulthood, he was punished by tattooing for a legal offense. Bu laughed with delight and said: people read me as one to be punished and then king, is it nearly so?
translations.idiomatic: As an adult he was sentenced to tattooing. Qing Bu laughed and said, So the reading that I would be punished and then become king is coming true.

sentences.zh: 人有聞者，共戲笑之。

translations.literal: Those who heard it all joked and laughed at him.
translations.idiomatic: People who heard him mocked him.

sentences.zh: 布以論輸驪山，驪山之徒數十萬人，布皆與其徒長豪桀交通，乃率其曹耦，亡之江中為群盜。

translations.literal: Bu was sentenced and transported to Lishan labor. Among Lishan's convict workers were several hundred thousand. Bu made ties with all gang leaders among them, then led his companions and fled to the river region as a bandit group.
translations.idiomatic: Qing Bu was sent as penal labor to Lishan, where there were hundreds of thousands of convicts. He befriended tough leaders among them, then escaped with his associates to become river-bandits.

sentences.zh: 陳勝之起也，布乃見番君，其眾數千人。

translations.literal: When Chen Sheng rose, Bu then went to see Lord Fan, with several thousand followers.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Sheng rose, Qing Bu joined Lord Fan with several thousand followers.

sentences.zh: 番君以女妻之。

translations.literal: Lord Fan gave his daughter to him as wife.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Fan gave him his daughter in marriage.

sentences.zh: 章邯之滅陳勝，破呂臣軍，布引兵北擊秦左右校，破之青波，引兵而東。

translations.literal: When Zhang Han destroyed Chen Sheng and broke Lü Chen's army, Bu led troops north to attack Qin's left and right colonels, defeated them at Qingbo, then led troops east.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhang Han destroyed Chen Sheng and defeated Lü Chen, Qing Bu led troops north against Qin detachments, beat them at Qingbo, and then moved east.

sentences.zh: 聞項梁定會稽，西度淮，布以兵屬梁。

translations.literal: Hearing Xiang Liang had settled Kuaiji and crossed west over the Huai, Bu attached his troops to Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Hearing that Xiang Liang had secured Kuaiji and crossed the Huai, Qing Bu joined him.

sentences.zh: 梁西擊景駒、秦嘉等，布常冠軍。

translations.literal: Liang attacked west against Jing Ju, Qin Jia, and others, and Bu often took first merit.
translations.idiomatic: As Xiang Liang campaigned west against Jing Ju, Qin Jia, and others, Qing Bu repeatedly took top honors.

sentences.zh: 項梁聞陳涉死，立楚懷王，以布為當陽君。

translations.literal: When Xiang Liang heard Chen She had died, he established King Huai of Chu and made Bu Lord of Dangyang.
translations.idiomatic: After hearing of Chen She's death, Xiang Liang established King Huai of Chu and made Qing Bu Lord of Dangyang.

sentences.zh: 項梁敗死，懷王與布及諸侯將皆聚彭城。

translations.literal: After Xiang Liang was defeated and died, King Huai, Bu, and the lords' generals all gathered at Pengcheng.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Liang was defeated and killed, King Huai, Qing Bu, and the allied commanders gathered at Pengcheng.

sentences.zh: 當是時，秦急圍趙，趙數使人請救懷王。

translations.literal: At that time Qin was urgently besieging Zhao, and Zhao repeatedly sent men to ask King Huai for relief.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Qin was pressing Zhao hard, and Zhao repeatedly asked King Huai for rescue.

sentences.zh: 懷王使宋義為上將，項籍與布皆屬之，北救趙。

translations.literal: King Huai made Song Yi supreme general, and Xiang Ji and Bu both were under him, going north to rescue Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai appointed Song Yi commander-in-chief, with Xiang Ji and Qing Bu under him, and sent them north to relieve Zhao.

sentences.zh: 及籍殺宋義河上，自立為上將軍，使布先涉河，擊秦軍，數有利。

translations.literal: When Ji killed Song Yi by the river and established himself as supreme general, he sent Bu first across the river to attack Qin troops, and there were repeatedly gains.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Ji killed Song Yi at the river and took command himself, he sent Qing Bu across first; Qing Bu won repeated successes against Qin.

sentences.zh: 籍乃悉引兵從之，遂破秦軍，降章邯等。

translations.literal: Ji then led all troops to follow him, and so broke Qin's army and accepted the surrender of Zhang Han and others.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Ji then brought up the full army, shattered Qin forces, and accepted Zhang Han's surrender.

sentences.zh: 楚兵常勝，功冠諸侯。

translations.literal: Chu troops were constantly victorious, and merit was foremost among the lords.
translations.idiomatic: Chu forces kept winning and stood first among the allied armies.

sentences.zh: 諸侯兵皆服屬楚者，以布數以少敗眾也。

translations.literal: The lords' armies all submitted to Chu because Bu repeatedly, with few, defeated many.
translations.idiomatic: The lords submitted to Chu in part because Qing Bu repeatedly defeated larger forces with smaller ones.

sentences.zh: 項籍之引兵西至新安，又使布等夜擊阬章邯秦卒二十餘萬人。

translations.literal: When Xiang Ji led troops west to Xin'an, he again sent Bu and others at night to attack and pit-bury more than two hundred thousand Qin soldiers of Zhang Han.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Ji marched west to Xin'an, he again sent Qing Bu and others to attack by night and bury alive over two hundred thousand surrendered Qin troops.

sentences.zh: 至關，不得入，又使布等先從間道破關下軍，遂得入。

translations.literal: When they reached the pass and could not enter, he again sent Bu and others first by side route to break the troops below the pass, and then they entered.
translations.idiomatic: At the passes, when entry was blocked, Xiang Ji again sent Qing Bu by side routes to smash the defenders below the pass, and they broke through.

sentences.zh: 至咸陽，布為前鋒。

translations.literal: When they reached Xianyang, Bu was the vanguard.
translations.idiomatic: At Xianyang, Qing Bu served as the vanguard.

sentences.zh: 項王封諸將，立布為九江王，都六。

translations.literal: When King Xiang enfeoffed the commanders, he established Bu as King of Jiujiang, with capital at Liu.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Yu enfeoffed his generals, he made Qing Bu king of Jiujiang, with his capital at Liu.

sentences.zh: 尊懷王為義帝，徙都長沙，乃陰令布擊之。

translations.literal: He honored King Huai as Emperor Yi and moved him to Changsha as capital, then secretly ordered Bu to attack him.
translations.idiomatic: He nominally honored King Huai as Emperor Yi and moved him to Changsha, then secretly ordered Qing Bu to attack him.

sentences.zh: 布使將追殺之郴。

translations.literal: Bu sent a general to pursue and kill him at Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Qing Bu sent a commander who pursued and killed Emperor Yi at Chen.

sentences.zh: 齊王田榮叛楚，項王往擊齊，徵兵九江，布稱病不往，遣將將數千人行。

translations.literal: When Tian Rong of Qi rebelled against Chu and King Xiang went to attack Qi, he requisitioned troops from Jiujiang. Bu claimed illness and did not go, sending a commander with several thousand men instead.
translations.idiomatic: When Tian Rong rebelled and Xiang Yu campaigned in Qi, he demanded troops from Jiujiang. Qing Bu claimed illness and sent only a few thousand under another commander.

sentences.zh: 漢之敗楚彭城，布又稱病不佐楚。

translations.literal: When Han defeated Chu at Pengcheng, Bu again claimed illness and did not assist Chu.
translations.idiomatic: When Han struck Chu at Pengcheng, Qing Bu again claimed illness and did not aid Chu.

sentences.zh: 項王由此怨布，數使使者譙讓召布，布愈恐，不敢往。

translations.literal: From this King Xiang resented Bu, repeatedly sending envoys to reproach and summon Bu. Bu became more afraid and did not dare go.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu resented him for this and repeatedly sent envoys to scold and summon him, but Qing Bu grew fearful and would not go.

sentences.zh: 項王方北憂齊、趙，西患漢，所與者獨布，又多其材，欲親用之，以故未擊。

translations.literal: King Xiang at that time in the north worried over Qi and Zhao, and in the west was troubled by Han. The only one allied with him was Bu, and he also valued Bu's talent and wished to use him personally, therefore he had not yet attacked.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Xiang Yu was stretched thin, troubled by Qi and Zhao in the north and Han in the west. Qing Bu was his only major ally, and Xiang Yu still valued his ability, so he held back from attacking him.

sentences.zh: 漢王與楚大戰彭城，不利，出梁地，至虞，謂左右曰：「

translations.literal: When the King of Han fought a great battle with Chu at Pengcheng and was unsuccessful, he left Liang territory and reached Yu, and told his attendants:
translations.idiomatic: After Liu Bang's defeat in the great battle at Pengcheng, he withdrew through Liang to Yu and told his attendants:

sentences.zh: 如彼等者，無足與計天下事者。」

translations.literal: Men like those are not enough to plan the affairs of all-under-Heaven with.
translations.idiomatic: Those men are not fit to plan empire-wide strategy.

sentences.zh: 謁者隨何進曰：「不審陛下所謂。」

translations.literal: Attendant Sui He stepped forward and said: I do not understand whom Your Majesty means.
translations.idiomatic: The courtier Sui He stepped forward and said, I do not understand whom Your Majesty means.

sentences.zh: 漢王曰：「孰能為我使淮南，使之發兵背楚，留項王於齊數月，我之取天下可以萬全。」

translations.literal: The King of Han said: Who can go as my envoy to Huainan, make him send troops and turn his back on Chu, and hold King Xiang in Qi for several months, so that my taking the empire can be fully secure?
translations.idiomatic: Liu Bang said, Who can go to Huainan and induce him to send troops against Chu? If Xiang Yu is tied down in Qi for a few months, my victory will be assured.

sentences.zh: 隨何曰：「臣請使之。」

translations.literal: Sui He said: I request to go as envoy.
translations.idiomatic: Sui He said, Let me go.

sentences.zh: 乃與二十人俱使淮南。

translations.literal: So he and twenty men together were sent as envoy to Huainan.
translations.idiomatic: He went to Huainan with twenty men.

sentences.zh: 至，太宰主之，三日不得見。

translations.literal: Upon arrival, the Grand Steward hosted him, and for three days he could not see the king.
translations.idiomatic: On arrival he was handled by the grand steward and could not see the king for three days.

sentences.zh: 隨何因說太宰曰：「王之不見何，必以楚為彊，以漢為弱，此臣之所為使。

translations.literal: Sui He then persuaded the Grand Steward, saying: The king's not seeing He must be because he takes Chu as strong and Han as weak. This is exactly what I am sent for.
translations.idiomatic: Sui He told the grand steward, The king refuses to see me because he thinks Chu is strong and Han is weak. That is exactly why I was sent.

sentences.zh: 使何得見，言之而是邪，是大王所欲聞也；

translations.literal: If He can gain audience and what he says is right, then this is what the great king wishes to hear;
translations.idiomatic: If I gain audience and speak correctly, I will be telling the king exactly what he wants to hear.

sentences.zh: 言之而非邪，使何等二十人伏斧質淮南巿，以明背漢而與楚也。」

translations.literal: If what he says is wrong, then let He and these twenty men lie under axe and block in the Huainan market, to make clear that Huainan turns against Han and joins Chu.
translations.idiomatic: If I am wrong, execute all twenty of us in Huainan's market and show openly that Huainan rejects Han and sides with Chu.

sentences.zh: 太宰乃言之王，王見之。

translations.literal: The Grand Steward then told this to the king, and the king granted audience.
translations.idiomatic: The grand steward relayed this, and the king agreed to see him.

sentences.zh: 隨何曰：「漢王使使臣敬進書大王御者，竊怪大王與楚何親也。」

translations.literal: Sui He said: The King of Han sent this envoy respectfully to present a letter before the great king. I privately wonder why the great king is so close with Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Sui He said, The King of Han has sent me to respectfully present a letter. I am puzzled why Your Majesty is so intimate with Chu.

sentences.zh: 淮南王曰：「寡人北鄉而臣事之。」

translations.literal: The King of Huainan said: I face north and serve him as subject.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Huainan said, I face north and serve Xiang Yu as a subject.

sentences.zh: 隨何曰：「大王與項王俱列為諸侯，北鄉而臣事之，必以楚為彊，可以託國也。

translations.literal: Sui He said: The great king and King Xiang are both listed as feudal lords; if you face north and serve him as subject, it must be because you take Chu as strong and able to entrust your state to.
translations.idiomatic: Sui He replied, You and Xiang Yu are both feudal kings. If you still face north as his subject, it must be because you believe Chu is stronger and your state can depend on it.

sentences.zh: 項王伐齊，身負版築，以為士卒先。

translations.literal: When King Xiang attacked Qi, he personally carried board and shovel earthwork, making himself first among the soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Yu attacked Qi, he took up labor gear himself and led from the front.

sentences.zh: 大王宜悉淮南之眾，身自將，為楚軍前鋒，今乃發四千人以助楚。

translations.literal: The great king should mobilize all Huainan's people, personally command, and serve as the vanguard of Chu's army. Yet now you only sent four thousand men to aid Chu.
translations.idiomatic: You should have mobilized all Huainan and led in person as Chu's vanguard. Instead, you sent only four thousand men.

sentences.zh: 夫北面而臣事人者，固若是乎？

translations.literal: For one who faces north and serves another as subject, should it really be like this?
translations.idiomatic: Is that how a true subordinate behaves?

sentences.zh: 夫漢王戰於彭城，項王未出齊也，大王宜埽淮南之眾，日夜會戰彭城下。

translations.literal: When the King of Han fought at Pengcheng, King Xiang had not yet left Qi. The great king should have swept up Huainan's troops and day and night joined battle below Pengcheng.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Bang fought at Pengcheng, Xiang Yu was still in Qi. You should have mobilized all Huainan and rushed day and night to fight at Pengcheng.

sentences.zh: 今撫萬人之眾，無一人渡淮者，陰拱而觀其孰勝。

translations.literal: Now you command a crowd of ten thousand men, yet not one has crossed the Huai. You secretly fold your arms and watch who wins.
translations.idiomatic: Now you command tens of thousands, yet not one man has crossed the Huai. You simply watch in silence to see who wins.

sentences.zh: 夫託國於人者，固若是乎？

translations.literal: For one who entrusts his state to another, is it truly like this?
translations.idiomatic: Is this how one behaves when entrusting his state to another power?

sentences.zh: 大王提空名以鄉楚，而欲厚自託，臣竊為大王不取也。

translations.literal: Great King, you carry an empty name of facing Chu, yet wish to rely on it deeply. I privately think this is not to be chosen.
translations.idiomatic: You keep only the nominal posture of siding with Chu while hoping to rely on it heavily. I think that is a bad choice.

sentences.zh: 然大王不背楚者，以漢為弱也。

translations.literal: Yet the reason Great King does not turn against Chu is because you take Han as weak.
translations.idiomatic: You still do not break with Chu only because you think Han is weak.

sentences.zh: 夫楚兵雖彊，天下負之以不義之名，以其背明約而殺義帝也。

translations.literal: Though Chu troops are strong, all under Heaven bear against it the name of unrighteousness, because it broke clear covenants and killed Emperor Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Chu may be militarily strong, but the whole realm condemns it as unrighteous for breaking solemn agreements and killing Emperor Yi.

sentences.zh: 然而楚王特以戰勝自彊。

translations.literal: Yet the King of Chu only takes battle victories as his source of strength.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu relies on battlefield wins alone.

sentences.zh: 漢王收諸侯，還守成皋、滎陽，下蜀、漢之粟，深溝壁壘，分卒守徼乘塞。

translations.literal: The King of Han gathers the lords, returns to guard Chenggao and Xingyang, brings down grain from Shu and Hanzhong, digs deep ditches and fortifies walls, and divides troops to guard border points and passes.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han has gathered the lords, held Chenggao and Xingyang, shipped grain from Shu and Hanzhong, fortified deeply, and stationed troops at strategic checkpoints.

sentences.zh: 楚人還兵，間以梁地，深入敵國八九百里，欲戰則不得，攻城則力不能，老弱轉糧千里之外。

translations.literal: Chu troops return and move through Liang territory, penetrating eight or nine hundred li into enemy state. If they wish battle, they cannot get it; if they attack cities, their strength cannot do it. Old and weak transport grain from a thousand li away.
translations.idiomatic: Chu's army has pushed eight or nine hundred li into hostile territory through Liang. It cannot force decisive battle, cannot easily take fortified cities, and must haul grain from far away with old and weak laborers.

sentences.zh: 楚兵至滎陽、成皋，漢堅守而不動，進則不得攻，退則不能解，故楚兵不足罷也。

translations.literal: When Chu troops reach Xingyang and Chenggao, Han firmly defends and does not move. Advancing, Chu cannot attack; retreating, Chu cannot disengage. Thus Chu troops are bound to wear out.
translations.idiomatic: At Xingyang and Chenggao, Han holds firm. Chu cannot break through if it advances, and cannot withdraw cleanly if it retreats. Its army will eventually grind down.

sentences.zh: 使楚兵勝漢，則諸侯自危懼而相救。

translations.literal: If Chu troops defeat Han, then the lords from fear for themselves will rescue each other.
translations.idiomatic: Even if Chu wins a battle, the other lords will fear for themselves and rally together.

sentences.zh: 夫楚之彊，適足以致天下之兵耳。

translations.literal: Chu's strength is only enough to draw the armies of all under Heaven against itself.
translations.idiomatic: Chu's strength is enough only to attract universal opposition.

sentences.zh: 故楚不如漢，其勢易見也。

translations.literal: Therefore Chu is not equal to Han; this tendency is easy to see.
translations.idiomatic: So Chu is at a strategic disadvantage to Han. The trend is obvious.

sentences.zh: 今大王不與萬全之漢，而自託於危亡之楚，臣竊為大王或之。

translations.literal: Now Great King does not join the all-secure Han, but instead entrusts himself to Chu in danger of ruin. I privately worry for Great King.
translations.idiomatic: So why abandon the safer side and tie yourself to a regime headed for danger? I worry for you.

sentences.zh: 臣非以淮南之兵足以亡楚也。

translations.literal: I do not mean that Huainan's troops are enough to destroy Chu.
translations.idiomatic: I am not saying Huainan alone can destroy Chu.

sentences.zh: 夫大王發兵而背楚，項王必留；

translations.literal: If Great King raises troops and turns against Chu, King Xiang will surely stay pinned down.
translations.idiomatic: But if you revolt against Chu, Xiang Yu will have to stay and deal with you.

sentences.zh: 留數月，漢之取天下可以萬全。

translations.literal: If he is held for several months, Han's taking of all under Heaven can be made fully secure.
translations.idiomatic: If he is tied down for a few months, Han's victory over the realm becomes virtually certain.

sentences.zh: 臣請與大王杖劍而歸漢王，漢王必裂地而分大王，又況淮南，必大王有也。

translations.literal: I ask that I and Great King take swords and return to the King of Han. The King of Han will surely split lands and share with Great King; much more so Huainan, Great King will certainly have it.
translations.idiomatic: Come with me to Han. Liu Bang will divide territory and reward you, and Huainan will certainly remain yours.

sentences.zh: 故漢王敬使使臣進愚計，願大王之留意也。」

translations.literal: Therefore the King of Han respectfully sent this envoy to present this foolish plan, wishing Great King to keep it in mind.
translations.idiomatic: That is why the King of Han respectfully sent me with this proposal. I hope Your Majesty will consider it.

sentences.zh: 淮南王曰：「請奉命。」

translations.literal: The King of Huainan said: I request to accept the command.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Huainan said, I will comply.

sentences.zh: 陰許叛楚與漢，未敢泄。

translations.literal: He secretly agreed to betray Chu and join Han, but did not yet dare reveal it.
translations.idiomatic: He secretly agreed to defect from Chu to Han but did not dare make it public yet.

sentences.zh: 楚使者在，方急責布發兵，隨何直入曰：「九江王已歸漢，楚何以得發兵！」

translations.literal: While the Chu envoy was present, urgently demanding Bu send troops, Sui He went straight in and said: The king of Jiujiang has already turned to Han. How can Chu demand troops!
translations.idiomatic: While a Chu envoy was pressing Ying Bu for troops, Sui He barged in and said, The king of Jiujiang has already joined Han. How can Chu still demand troops?

sentences.zh: 布愕然。

translations.literal: Bu was stunned.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu was stunned.

sentences.zh: 楚使者起，何因說布曰：「事已搆，獨可遂殺楚使，毋使歸，而疾走漢并力。」

translations.literal: The Chu envoy rose. He then advised Bu: the matter is already formed. Only one path: kill the Chu envoy so he cannot return, and quickly go to Han to join forces.
translations.idiomatic: As the Chu envoy stood up, Sui He urged Ying Bu: things are already exposed. Kill the envoy, prevent him from returning, and hurry to join Han.

sentences.zh: 布曰：「如使者教。」

translations.literal: Bu said: as the envoy teaches.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu said, I will do as you advise.

sentences.zh: 因起兵而攻楚。

translations.literal: He thereupon raised troops and attacked Chu.
translations.idiomatic: He then raised troops and attacked Chu.

sentences.zh: 楚使項聲、龍且攻淮南，項王留而攻下邑。

translations.literal: Chu sent Xiang Sheng and Long Ju to attack Huainan. King Xiang stayed to attack Xiayi.
translations.idiomatic: Chu sent Xiang Sheng and Long Ju against Huainan, while Xiang Yu stayed behind to attack Xiayi.

sentences.zh: 數月，龍且攻淮南，破布軍。

translations.literal: After several months, Long Ju attacked Huainan and broke Bu's army.
translations.idiomatic: After several months, Long Ju defeated Ying Bu's army in Huainan.

sentences.zh: 布欲引兵走漢，恐項王擊之，故間行與隨何俱歸漢。

translations.literal: Bu wished to lead troops and run to Han, but feared King Xiang would strike him, so he went by side route and with Sui He returned to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu wanted to flee to Han, but fearing interception by Xiang Yu, he took secret routes back with Sui He.

sentences.zh: 至，漢王方踞床洗，而召布入見。

translations.literal: On arrival, the King of Han was just squatting on a couch washing, and summoned Bu to audience.
translations.idiomatic: When he arrived, Liu Bang was squatting and washing when he summoned Ying Bu in.

sentences.zh: 布大怒，悔來，欲自殺。

translations.literal: Bu was greatly angry, regretted coming, and wished to kill himself.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu was furious at the slight, regretted coming, and wanted to kill himself.

sentences.zh: 出就舍，張御食飲從官如漢王居，布又大喜過望。

translations.literal: When he went out to his lodging, he saw furnishings, food, drink, and attendants like those of the King of Han's own quarters. Bu was again greatly delighted beyond expectation.
translations.idiomatic: But when he reached his lodging, it was furnished and provisioned exactly like the king's own quarters, with full attendants. Ying Bu was delighted beyond expectation.

sentences.zh: 於是乃使人之九江。

translations.literal: Thereupon he then sent men to Jiujiang.
translations.idiomatic: He then sent men back to Jiujiang.

sentences.zh: 楚已使項伯收九江兵，盡殺布妻子。

translations.literal: Chu had already sent Xiang Bo to gather Jiujiang troops and had killed all Bu's wife and children.
translations.idiomatic: Chu had already sent Xiang Bo to seize Jiujiang troops and had killed Ying Bu's wife and children.

sentences.zh: 布使者頗得故人幸臣，將眾數千人歸漢。

translations.literal: Bu's envoys gained quite a few old acquaintances and favored retainers, and led several thousand men back to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu's envoys managed to rally old associates and favored retainers, bringing several thousand men back to Han.

sentences.zh: 漢益分布兵而與俱北，收兵至成皋。

translations.literal: Han further divided troops to Bu and together they went north, gathering troops up to Chenggao.
translations.idiomatic: Han reinforced Ying Bu with additional troops, and together they moved north, assembling forces at Chenggao.

sentences.zh: 四年秋七月，立布為淮南王，與擊項籍。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month of year four, Bu was established as King of Huainan and together attacked Xiang Ji.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn, year four, Ying Bu was formally made king of Huainan, and joined the campaign against Xiang Yu.

sentences.zh: 布使人之九江，得數縣。

translations.literal: Bu sent men to Jiujiang and obtained several counties.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu sent men into Jiujiang and recovered several counties.

sentences.zh: 五年，布與劉賈入九江，誘大司馬周殷，殷反楚。

translations.literal: In year five, Bu and Liu Jia entered Jiujiang and enticed Grand Marshal Zhou Yin, and Yin turned against Chu.
translations.idiomatic: In year five, Ying Bu and Liu Jia entered Jiujiang and won over Grand Marshal Zhou Yin, who then defected from Chu.

sentences.zh: 遂舉九江兵與漢擊楚，破垓下。

translations.literal: They then raised Jiujiang troops and with Han attacked Chu, defeating at Gaixia.
translations.idiomatic: They then mobilized Jiujiang's troops and joined Han in the final victory over Chu at Gaixia.

sentences.zh: 項籍死，上置酒對眾折隨何曰腐儒，「為天下安用腐儒哉！」

translations.literal: After Xiang Ji died, the emperor held a feast and before the crowd mocked Sui He as a rotten scholar: for all under Heaven, what use is a rotten scholar!
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Yu's death, the emperor held a feast and publicly mocked Sui He as a useless pedant: What use are pedants in winning an empire?

sentences.zh: 隨何跪曰：「夫陛下引兵攻彭城，楚王未去齊也，陛下發步卒五萬人，騎五千，能以取淮南乎？」

translations.literal: Sui He knelt and said: When Your Majesty led troops to attack Pengcheng and the King of Chu had not yet left Qi, if Your Majesty had sent fifty thousand infantry and five thousand cavalry, could you have taken Huainan?
translations.idiomatic: Sui He knelt and asked: When you attacked Pengcheng and Xiang Yu was still in Qi, could fifty thousand infantry and five thousand cavalry have taken Huainan?

sentences.zh: 曰：「不能。」

translations.literal: He said: cannot.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, No.

sentences.zh: 隨何曰：「

translations.literal: Sui He said:
translations.idiomatic: Sui He said:

sentences.zh: 陛下使何與二十人使淮南，如陛下之意，是何之功賢於步卒數萬，騎五千也。

translations.literal: If Your Majesty sent He and twenty men as envoys to Huainan, and it matched Your Majesty's intent, then He's merit is worthier than tens of thousands of infantry and five thousand cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: If sending me with twenty men achieved exactly your strategic goal in Huainan, then my merit exceeds that of those fifty thousand infantry and five thousand cavalry.

sentences.zh: 然陛下謂何腐儒，『為天下安用腐儒』，何也？」

translations.literal: Yet Your Majesty calls He a rotten scholar and says what use is a rotten scholar for all under Heaven. Why?
translations.idiomatic: So why call me a useless scholar?

sentences.zh: 上曰：「

translations.literal: The emperor said:
translations.idiomatic: The emperor replied:

sentences.zh: 吾方圖子之功。」

translations.literal: I was just now intending to reckon your merit.
translations.idiomatic: I was just about to reward your achievement.

sentences.zh: 乃以隨何為護軍中尉。

translations.literal: Then he made Sui He Commandant of the Army Protector.
translations.idiomatic: He then appointed Sui He as Commandant of the Army Protector.

sentences.zh: 布遂剖符為淮南王，都六，九江、廬江、衡山、豫章郡皆屬焉。

translations.literal: Bu then received split tally and became King of Huainan, with capital at Liu. Jiujiang, Lujiang, Hengshan, and Yuzhang commanderies all belonged to him.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu then received the royal tally as king of Huainan, with his capital at Liu, ruling the commanderies of Jiujiang, Lujiang, Hengshan, and Yuzhang.

sentences.zh: 六年，朝陳。

translations.literal: In year six, he came to court at Chen.
translations.idiomatic: In year six, he came to court at Chen.

sentences.zh: 七年，朝雒陽。

translations.literal: In year seven, he came to court at Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: In year seven, he came to court at Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 九年，朝長安。

translations.literal: In year nine, he came to court at Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: In year nine, he came to court at Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 十一年，高后誅淮陰侯，布因心恐。

translations.literal: In year eleven, Empress Gao executed the Marquis of Huaiyin, and Bu therefore became fearful in heart.
translations.idiomatic: In year eleven, Empress Lü executed the Marquis of Huaiyin, and Ying Bu became deeply afraid.

sentences.zh: 夏，漢誅梁王彭越，盛其醢以遍賜諸侯。

translations.literal: In summer, Han executed King Peng Yue of Liang and made his body into minced meat and widely bestowed it to the lords.
translations.idiomatic: That summer Han executed Peng Yue and distributed minced flesh from his body to the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 至淮南，淮南王方獵，見醢，因大恐，陰令人部聚兵，候伺旁郡警急。

translations.literal: When it reached Huainan, the King of Huainan was out hunting. Seeing the minced flesh, he became greatly afraid, and secretly ordered men to assemble troops and watch neighboring commanderies for urgent alarms.
translations.idiomatic: When the package reached Huainan, Ying Bu was hunting. Seeing it, he was terrified and secretly ordered troop mobilization, watching nearby commanderies for signs of emergency.

sentences.zh: 布有所幸姬病，就醫。

translations.literal: Bu had a favored consort who fell ill and went to seek treatment.
translations.idiomatic: A favored concubine of Ying Bu fell ill and went for medical treatment.

sentences.zh: 醫家與中大夫賁赫對門，赫乃厚餽遺，從姬飲醫家。

translations.literal: The doctor's house faced that of Mid-Grandee Ben He. He then gave heavy gifts and accompanied the consort drinking at the doctor's house.
translations.idiomatic: The doctor's house stood opposite Mid-Grandee Ben He, who sent lavish gifts and joined the woman drinking there.

sentences.zh: 姬侍王，從容語次，譽赫長者也。

translations.literal: When the consort attended the king, in casual conversation she praised He as a worthy elder.
translations.idiomatic: While attending the king, she casually praised Ben He as a fine and honorable man.

sentences.zh: 王怒曰：「女安從知之？」

translations.literal: The king angrily said: How do you know him?
translations.idiomatic: The king angrily asked, How do you know him?

sentences.zh: 具道，王疑與亂。

translations.literal: She fully explained it. The king suspected illicit relations.
translations.idiomatic: She told him everything, and the king suspected an affair.

sentences.zh: 赫恐，稱病。

translations.literal: He was frightened and claimed illness.
translations.idiomatic: Ben He became afraid and claimed illness.

sentences.zh: 王愈怒，欲捕赫。

translations.literal: The king became even angrier and wished to arrest He.
translations.idiomatic: The king grew even angrier and wanted him arrested.

sentences.zh: 赫上變事，乘傳詣長安。

translations.literal: He submitted a memorial reporting the matter and rode relay carriage to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Ben He rushed to Chang'an by relay post and reported the matter.

sentences.zh: 布使人追，不及。

translations.literal: Bu sent men to pursue him, but they could not catch him.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu sent pursuers, but they failed to catch him.

sentences.zh: 赫至，上變，言布謀反有端，可先未發誅也。

translations.literal: When He arrived, he reported change, saying Bu's plotting rebellion had signs and he could be executed before it erupted.
translations.idiomatic: On reaching court, Ben He reported that Ying Bu was showing clear signs of rebellion and could be eliminated before open revolt.

sentences.zh: 上以其書語蕭相國，蕭相國曰：「布不宜有此，恐仇怨妄誣之。

translations.literal: The emperor took his letter and discussed with Chancellor Xiao. Chancellor Xiao said: Bu should not have this. I fear enemies with grudges are falsely accusing him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor showed the letter to Chancellor Xiao, who said, This seems unlikely. I fear a false accusation driven by private grudges.

sentences.zh: 請繫赫，使人微驗淮南王。」

translations.literal: Please arrest He and send men to secretly verify the King of Huainan.
translations.idiomatic: Detain Ben He and send men to verify Huainan quietly.

sentences.zh: 布見赫以罪亡上變，已疑其言國陰事，漢使又來，頗有所驗，遂族赫家，發兵反。

translations.literal: Bu saw that He had fled under accusation and reported upward, and already suspected he had spoken of secret state matters. Han envoys came again and did some verification. So he exterminated He's family and raised troops in rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: When Ying Bu learned that Ben He had fled and denounced him, and when Han investigators arrived to verify details, he assumed exposure was complete. He exterminated Ben He's family and raised rebellion.

sentences.zh: 反書聞，上乃赦赫，以為將軍。

translations.literal: When news of rebellion reached court, the emperor then pardoned He and made him a general.
translations.idiomatic: Once revolt was confirmed, the emperor pardoned Ben He and appointed him general.

sentences.zh: 召諸侯問：「布反，為之柰何？」

translations.literal: He summoned the lords and asked: Bu has rebelled, what to do about it?
translations.idiomatic: He summoned the lords and asked, Ying Bu has rebelled. What should be done?

sentences.zh: 皆曰：「發兵阬豎子耳，何能為！」

translations.literal: All said: Send troops and bury this brat, what can he do!
translations.idiomatic: They all answered, Send troops and crush the wretch. What can he do?

sentences.zh: 汝陰侯滕公以問其客薛公，薛公曰：「是固當反。」

translations.literal: The Marquis of Ruyin, Lord Teng, used this to ask his guest Lord Xue. Lord Xue said: this was certainly bound to rebel.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Teng of Ruyin asked his adviser Xue Gong, who said, This rebellion was inevitable.

sentences.zh: 滕公曰：「上裂地而封之，疏爵而貴之，南面而立萬乘之主，其反何也？」

translations.literal: Lord Teng said: The emperor split lands and enfeoffed him, granted rank and honored him, setting him up south-facing as a lord of ten-thousand chariots. Why would he rebel?
translations.idiomatic: Lord Teng said, The emperor granted him land, rank, and royal status. Why would he rebel?

sentences.zh: 薛公曰：「前年殺彭越，往年殺韓信，三人皆同功一體之人也。

translations.literal: Lord Xue said: The year before last Peng Yue was killed; last year Han Xin was killed. These three were all men of equal merit and one body.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Gong said, Peng Yue was killed one year, Han Xin the next. Those three were men of the same class of merit.

sentences.zh: 自疑禍及身，故反耳。」

translations.literal: He himself suspected disaster would reach his own body, and therefore rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu feared the same fate would reach him, so he rebelled.

sentences.zh: 滕公言之上曰：「臣客故楚令尹薛公，其人有籌策，可問。」

translations.literal: Lord Teng told this to the emperor, saying: your servant's guest, former Chu Lingyin Lord Xue, has strategic plans and can be consulted.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Teng reported this to the emperor and said, My adviser Xue Gong, former Lingyin of Chu, has strategic insight and should be consulted.

sentences.zh: 上乃見問薛公，對曰：「布反不足怪也。

translations.literal: The emperor then met and questioned Lord Xue, who replied: Bu's rebellion is not enough to be strange.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor summoned Xue Gong, who replied, Ying Bu's rebellion is no surprise.

sentences.zh: 使布出於上計，山東非漢之有也；

translations.literal: If Bu chooses the highest plan, the lands east of the mountains will not belong to Han;
translations.idiomatic: If Ying Bu follows a top-tier strategy, all territory east of the passes will be lost to Han.

sentences.zh: 出於中計，勝負之數未可知也；

translations.literal: If he chooses the middle plan, the outcome of victory and defeat cannot yet be known;
translations.idiomatic: If he chooses a middle strategy, the outcome is uncertain.

sentences.zh: 出於下計，陛下安枕而臥矣。」

translations.literal: If he chooses the lowest plan, Your Majesty can sleep with pillow secure.
translations.idiomatic: If he chooses a poor strategy, Your Majesty can sleep soundly.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「

translations.literal: The emperor said:
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked:

sentences.zh: 何謂上計？」

translations.literal: What is called highest plan?
translations.idiomatic: What is the top strategy?

sentences.zh: 薛公對曰：「東取吳，西取楚，并齊取魯，傳檄燕、趙，固守其所，山東非漢之有也。」

translations.literal: Lord Xue replied: Take Wu in the east, take Chu in the west, absorb Qi and take Lu, send proclamations to Yan and Zhao, and hold firmly what he has; then lands east of the mountains are not Han's.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Gong said, Seize Wu to the east and Chu to the west, absorb Qi and Lu, send calls to arms to Yan and Zhao, and hold those gains. Then Han loses the east.

sentences.zh: 「何謂中計？」

translations.literal: What is called middle plan?
translations.idiomatic: What is the middle strategy?

sentences.zh: 「東取吳，西取楚，并韓取魏，據敖倉之粟，塞成皋之險，勝敗之數未可知也。」

translations.literal: Take Wu in the east, take Chu in the west, merge Han and seize Wei, occupy grain at Ao granary, and block the pass at Chenggao. The odds of victory and defeat cannot yet be known.
translations.idiomatic: Take Wu and Chu, absorb Han and Wei, hold the Ao granary, and block Chenggao. Then victory or defeat remains uncertain.

sentences.zh: 「何謂下計？」

translations.literal: What is called lower plan?
translations.idiomatic: What is the lower strategy?

sentences.zh: 「東取吳，西取下蔡，歸重於越，身歸長沙，陛下安枕而臥，漢無事矣。」

translations.literal: Take Wu in the east, take Xiacai in the west, send heavy baggage to Yue, and personally return to Changsha. Then Your Majesty can sleep secure and Han will have no trouble.
translations.idiomatic: Take Wu and Xiacai, send baggage away to Yue territory, and retreat personally to Changsha. Then Your Majesty can rest easily and Han faces no serious threat.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「是計將安出？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: Which plan will he choose?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked, Which one will he choose?

sentences.zh: 薛公曰：「

translations.literal: Lord Xue said:
translations.idiomatic: Xue Gong answered:

sentences.zh: 出下計。」

translations.literal: He will choose the lower plan.
translations.idiomatic: He will choose the lowest strategy.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「胡為廢上計而出下計？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: Why abandon the highest plan and choose the lower plan?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked, Why would he abandon the best strategy for the worst?

sentences.zh: 薛公曰：「布故驪山之徒也，致萬乘之主，此皆為身，不顧後為百姓萬世慮者也，故出下計。」

translations.literal: Lord Xue said: Bu was originally one of the Lishan convicts. To reach lordship of ten-thousand chariots, all this was for his own person, not for later concern for the people for ten thousand generations. Therefore he chooses the lower plan.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Gong said, Ying Bu began as a Lishan convict. His rise to kingship was for his own survival, not long-term care for the people. So he will choose the lesser strategy.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「善。」

translations.literal: The emperor said: Good.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, Good.

sentences.zh: 封薛公千戶。

translations.literal: He enfeoffed Lord Xue with one thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: He granted Xue Gong a fief of one thousand households.

sentences.zh: 遂發兵自將東擊布。

translations.literal: Then he raised troops and personally led east to attack Bu.
translations.idiomatic: He then mobilized the army and personally marched east against Ying Bu.

sentences.zh: 布之初反，謂其將曰：「上老矣，厭兵，必不能來。

translations.literal: When Bu first rebelled, he told his generals: the emperor is old and weary of war; he surely cannot come.
translations.idiomatic: When Ying Bu first rebelled, he told his generals, The emperor is old and tired of campaigning. He will not come himself.

sentences.zh: 使諸將，諸將獨患淮陰、彭越，今已死，餘不足畏。」

translations.literal: If he sends generals, the generals only feared Huaiyin and Peng Yue. Now they are dead, the rest are not enough to fear.
translations.idiomatic: If he sends generals, the only men they truly feared were Han Xin and Peng Yue. Both are dead now, so no one else matters.

sentences.zh: 故遂反。

translations.literal: Therefore he then rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: That is why he rebelled.

sentences.zh: 果如薛公揣之，東擊荊，荊王劉賈走死富陵。

translations.literal: It indeed matched Lord Xue's estimate: he attacked Jing in the east, and King Liu Jia of Jing fled and died at Fuling.
translations.idiomatic: Exactly as Xue predicted, Ying Bu struck Jing first; king Liu Jia fled and died at Fuling.

sentences.zh: 盡劫其兵，度淮擊楚。

translations.literal: He seized all its troops and crossed the Huai to attack Chu.
translations.idiomatic: He took all their troops and crossed the Huai to attack Chu territory.

sentences.zh: 楚發兵與戰徐、僮間，為三軍，欲以相救為奇。

translations.literal: Chu sent troops and fought between Xu and Tong, dividing into three armies, wanting to use mutual rescue as a stratagem.
translations.idiomatic: Chu deployed three separate armies to fight in the Xu-Tong region, hoping they could support one another.

sentences.zh: 或說楚將曰：「布善用兵，民素畏之。

translations.literal: Someone advised the Chu general: Bu is skilled at using troops; the people have long feared him.
translations.idiomatic: Someone warned the Chu commander, Ying Bu is an excellent commander and has long inspired fear.

sentences.zh: 且兵法，諸侯自戰其地為散地。

translations.literal: Moreover, military method says: when lords fight in their own land, it is dispersive ground.
translations.idiomatic: And military doctrine says that fighting on home ground makes troops prone to dispersion.

sentences.zh: 今別為三，彼敗吾一，餘皆走，安能相救！」

translations.literal: Now split into three, if he defeats one of ours, the rest all run. How can they rescue each other?
translations.idiomatic: If we divide into three and he breaks one, the others will flee. How will they reinforce each other then?

sentences.zh: 不聽。

translations.literal: He did not listen.
translations.idiomatic: The advice was ignored.

sentences.zh: 布果破其一軍，二軍散走。

translations.literal: Bu indeed broke one of their armies, and the other two scattered and fled.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu did break one army, and the other two collapsed and ran.

sentences.zh: 遂西，與上兵遇蘄西，會诓。

translations.literal: He then went west and met the emperor's troops west of Qi, at Kuang.
translations.idiomatic: He moved west and met the emperor's forces west of Qi, at Kuang.

sentences.zh: 布兵精甚，上乃壁庸城，望布軍置陳如項籍軍。

translations.literal: Bu's troops were very elite. The emperor then fortified Yongcheng and observed Bu's army setting formations like Xiang Ji's army.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu's troops were highly disciplined. The emperor fortified Yongcheng and saw that Bu's formations resembled Xiang Yu's.

sentences.zh: 上惡之，與布相望見，隃謂布「何苦而反？」

translations.literal: The emperor disliked this. Facing Bu at a distance, he said to Bu: Why suffer and rebel?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor disliked what he saw and shouted to Bu across the lines, Why put yourself through this rebellion?

sentences.zh: 布曰：「欲為帝耳。」

translations.literal: Bu said: I only want to be emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu replied, I want to be emperor.

sentences.zh: 上怒罵之，遂戰，破布軍。

translations.literal: The emperor angrily cursed him, then fought and broke Bu's army.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor cursed him in rage, then attacked and defeated Bu's army.

sentences.zh: 布走度淮，數止戰，不利，與百餘人走江南。

translations.literal: Bu fled across the Huai. Several times he halted to fight, but without advantage. He fled south of the Yangtze with over one hundred men.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu fled across the Huai and fought several delaying actions, all unsuccessfully, then escaped south of the Yangtze with a little over a hundred followers.

sentences.zh: 布舊與番君婚，故長沙哀王使人誘布，偽與俱亡，走越，布信而隨至番陽。

translations.literal: Bu had old marriage ties with Lord Fan, so King Ai of Changsha sent men to lure Bu, pretending to flee together to Yue. Bu believed it and followed to Poyang.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Bu had old marriage ties with the house of Fan, so King Ai of Changsha sent men to lure him, pretending they would flee together into Yue. Ying Bu believed them and followed to Poyang.

sentences.zh: 番陽人殺布茲鄉，遂滅之。

translations.literal: People of Poyang killed Bu at Zixiang, and thus destroyed him.
translations.idiomatic: Men of Poyang killed Ying Bu at Zixiang, ending him.

sentences.zh: 封賁赫為列侯，將率封者六人。

translations.literal: Ben He was enfeoffed as marquis; six commanders in all were enfeoffed.
translations.idiomatic: Ben He was granted a marquisate, and six commanders in total were enfeoffed.

sentences.zh: 盧綰，豐人也，與高祖同里。

translations.literal: Lu Wan was a man of Feng and from the same village as Gaozu.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Wan was from Feng, from the same village as Gaozu.

sentences.zh: 綰親與高祖太上皇相愛，及生男，高祖、綰同日生，里中持羊酒賀兩家。

translations.literal: Wan's family was close and affectionate with Gaozu and the Supreme Emperor. When sons were born, Gaozu and Wan were born on the same day, and people of the village brought sheep and wine to congratulate both families.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Wan's family was close to Gaozu's household. Lu Wan and Gaozu were even born on the same day, and villagers celebrated both births with sheep and wine.

sentences.zh: 及高祖、綰壯，學書，又相愛也。

translations.literal: When Gaozu and Wan grew up, they studied writing and were again close and affectionate.
translations.idiomatic: As they grew up and studied together, their friendship remained very close.

sentences.zh: 里中嘉兩家親相愛，生子同日，壯又相愛，復賀羊酒。

translations.literal: The villagers admired how close the two families were, their sons born on the same day and later close as adults, and again celebrated with sheep and wine.
translations.idiomatic: The villagers admired this bond and celebrated again when the two, born on the same day, remained close in adulthood.

sentences.zh: 高祖為布衣時，有吏事避宅，綰常隨上下。

translations.literal: When Gaozu was a commoner, if he had official matters and stayed away from home, Wan often followed him in and out.
translations.idiomatic: When Gaozu was still a commoner handling official errands, Lu Wan often accompanied him everywhere.

sentences.zh: 及高祖初起沛，綰以客從，入漢為將軍，常侍中。

translations.literal: When Gaozu first rose at Pei, Wan followed as a retainer; after entering Han he became a general and often served in attendance.
translations.idiomatic: When Gaozu first rose at Pei, Lu Wan followed him as a retainer. After entering Han, he became a general and an attendant at court.

sentences.zh: 從東擊項籍，以太尉常從，出入臥內，衣被食飲賞賜，群臣莫敢望。

translations.literal: He followed east attacking Xiang Ji. As Grand Commandant he was always with the emperor, entering and leaving the bedchamber. Clothing, bedding, food, drink, and rewards - none among ministers dared compare.
translations.idiomatic: He campaigned east against Xiang Yu and, as grand commandant, enjoyed exceptional intimacy with Gaozu, with access to the private quarters and unmatched rewards.

sentences.zh: 雖蕭、曹等，特以事見禮，至其親幸，莫及綰者。

translations.literal: Even Xiao and Cao and others were only ceremonially received for affairs. In personal favor, none reached Wan.
translations.idiomatic: Even men like Xiao He and Cao Shen were honored mainly in official matters; in personal favor, none matched Lu Wan.

sentences.zh: 封為長安侯。

translations.literal: He was enfeoffed as Marquis of Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: He was enfeoffed as Marquis of Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 長安，故咸陽也。

translations.literal: Chang'an was former Xianyang.
translations.idiomatic: Chang'an was the old city of Xianyang.

sentences.zh: 項籍死，使綰別將，與劉賈擊臨江王共尉，還，從擊燕王臧荼，皆破平。

translations.literal: After Xiang Ji died, the emperor sent Wan on separate command with Liu Jia to attack Gong Wei, king of Linjiang. Returning, he followed to attack Zang Tu, king of Yan. All were defeated and pacified.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Yu's death, Lu Wan and Liu Jia defeated Gong Wei of Linjiang, then helped crush Zang Tu of Yan.

sentences.zh: 時諸侯非劉氏而王者七人。

translations.literal: At that time there were seven kings among the lords who were not of the Liu clan.
translations.idiomatic: At that time seven non-Liu kings still remained.

sentences.zh: 上欲王綰，為群臣觖望。

translations.literal: The emperor wished to make Wan king, causing disappointment among the ministers.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor wanted to elevate Lu Wan to kingship, which stirred resentment among ministers.

sentences.zh: 及虜臧荼，乃下詔，詔諸將相列侯擇群臣有功者以為燕王。

translations.literal: After capturing Zang Tu, he issued an edict ordering generals, chancellors, and marquises to choose among meritorious ministers one to be king of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: After Zang Tu was captured, the emperor issued an edict asking top officials to nominate a meritorious man as king of Yan.

sentences.zh: 群臣知上欲王綰，皆曰：「太尉長安侯盧綰常從平定天下，功最多，可王。」

translations.literal: The ministers knew the emperor wanted Wan, and all said: Grand Commandant and Marquis of Chang'an Lu Wan has long followed in pacifying all under Heaven and has the most merit; he can be king.
translations.idiomatic: Knowing the emperor's preference, the ministers all recommended Lu Wan, citing his long service and great merit.

sentences.zh: 上乃立綰為燕王。

translations.literal: The emperor then established Wan as king of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then made Lu Wan king of Yan.

sentences.zh: 諸侯得幸莫如燕王者。

translations.literal: Among the lords, none received favor like the king of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: No other feudal king enjoyed such favor.

sentences.zh: 綰立六年，以陳豨事見疑而敗。

translations.literal: Wan ruled six years, then because of the Chen Xi affair was suspected and ruined.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Wan ruled for six years, then was undone after being implicated in the Chen Xi affair.

sentences.zh: 豨者，宛句人也，不知始所以得從。

translations.literal: As for Xi, he was a man of Wanju; it is unknown how he first came to follow.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Xi was from Wanju; it is unclear how he first entered service.

sentences.zh: 及韓王信反入匈奴，上至平城還，豨以郎中封為列侯，以趙相國將監趙、代邊，邊兵皆屬焉。

translations.literal: When Han king Xin rebelled and entered the Xiongnu, the emperor went to Pingcheng and returned. Xi, as palace gentleman, was enfeoffed marquis, and as Zhao chancellor commanded and supervised the Zhao and Dai borders. Border troops all belonged to him.
translations.idiomatic: After Han Xin's rebellion into Xiongnu and the emperor's return from Pingcheng, Chen Xi was enfeoffed as marquis and put in charge of the Zhao-Dai frontier, with border troops under his command.

sentences.zh: 豨少時，常稱慕魏公子，及將守邊，招致賓客。

translations.literal: In youth Xi often claimed admiration for the Prince of Wei. When about to guard the frontier, he recruited and gathered guests.
translations.idiomatic: In youth Chen Xi admired the famed prince of Wei. When assigned to the frontier, he gathered a large retinue of followers.

sentences.zh: 常告過趙，賓客隨之者千餘乘，邯鄲官舍皆滿。

translations.literal: Whenever passing through Zhao, over a thousand carriages of guests followed him, and official lodgings in Handan were all full.
translations.idiomatic: When he passed through Zhao, more than a thousand carriages of followers accompanied him, filling the official lodgings at Handan.

sentences.zh: 豨所以待客，如布衣交，皆出客下。

translations.literal: How Xi treated guests was like friendship among commoners; he always placed himself below the guests.
translations.idiomatic: He treated guests as equals in plain-clothes friendship, always placing himself beneath them.

sentences.zh: 趙相周昌乃求入見上，具言豨賓客盛，擅兵於外，恐有變。

translations.literal: Zhao chancellor Zhou Chang then sought audience with the emperor and fully reported that Xi's guests were numerous and that he controlled troops outside, fearing there might be change.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Chang warned the emperor that Chen Xi's private following was too large and that he commanded troops away from court, creating danger.

sentences.zh: 上令人覆案豨客居代者諸為不法事，多連引豨。

translations.literal: The emperor had people re-investigate Xi's guests residing in Dai. Many illegal acts connected and implicated Xi.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ordered a reinvestigation of Chen Xi's followers in Dai, and many crimes were traced back to him.

sentences.zh: 豨恐，陰令客通使王黃、曼丘臣所。

translations.literal: Xi was afraid and secretly had guests carry messages to Wang Huang and Manqiu Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Xi grew afraid and secretly sent contacts to Wang Huang and Manqiu Chen.

sentences.zh: 漢十年秋，太上皇崩，上因是召豨。

translations.literal: In autumn of Han year ten, the Supreme Emperor died, and the emperor used this to summon Xi.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Han year ten, after the Supreme Emperor died, the emperor summoned Chen Xi.

sentences.zh: 豨稱病，遂與王黃等反，自立為代王，劫略趙、代。

translations.literal: Xi claimed illness, then rebelled with Wang Huang and others, setting himself up as king of Dai and plundering Zhao and Dai.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Xi claimed illness, then rebelled with Wang Huang and others, proclaimed himself king of Dai, and ravaged Zhao and Dai.

sentences.zh: 上聞，乃赦吏民為豨所詿誤劫略者。

translations.literal: When the emperor heard, he pardoned officials and people who had been implicated or coerced by Xi.
translations.idiomatic: On hearing this, the emperor pardoned those officials and commoners whom Chen Xi had implicated or coerced.

sentences.zh: 上自擊豨，破之。

translations.literal: The emperor personally attacked Xi and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor personally campaigned and defeated Chen Xi.

sentences.zh: 語在高紀。

translations.literal: The account is in the Annals of Gaozu.
translations.idiomatic: The details are in the Annals of Gaozu.

sentences.zh: 初，上如邯鄲擊豨，燕王綰亦擊其東北。

translations.literal: At first, when the emperor went to Handan to attack Xi, King Wan of Yan also attacked Xi's northeast.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor moved on Handan against Chen Xi, king Lu Wan of Yan also attacked Chen Xi's northeastern flank.

sentences.zh: 豨使王黃求救匈奴，綰亦使其臣張勝使匈奴，言豨等軍破。

translations.literal: Xi sent Wang Huang to seek help from the Xiongnu. Wan also sent his minister Zhang Sheng as envoy to Xiongnu, saying Xi's forces had been broken.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Xi sent Wang Huang to seek Xiongnu aid, while Lu Wan sent his own minister Zhang Sheng to the Xiongnu reporting Chen Xi's defeat.

sentences.zh: 勝至胡，故燕王臧荼子衍亡在胡，見勝曰：「公所以重於燕者，以習胡事也。

translations.literal: When Sheng arrived among the Hu, Yan, son of former king Zang Tu of Yan, who had fled and was among the Hu, saw Sheng and said: the reason you are valued in Yan is because you know Hu affairs.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhang Sheng reached the steppe, Yan, son of former Yan king Zang Tu, told him: You are valued in Yan because you understand relations with the Hu.

sentences.zh: 燕所以久存者，以諸侯數反，兵連不決也。

translations.literal: The reason Yan has long survived is because the lords often rebel, and warfare remains unresolved.
translations.idiomatic: Yan has survived so long only because rebellions among the lords have kept the wars unsettled.

sentences.zh: 今公為燕欲急滅豨等，豨等已盡，次亦至燕，公等亦且為虜矣。

translations.literal: Now you for Yan wish to quickly destroy Xi and others. Once Xi and others are all gone, next it will also come to Yan, and you too will become captives.
translations.idiomatic: If you now help Yan quickly wipe out Chen Xi, Yan will be next, and you yourselves will become prisoners.

sentences.zh: 公何不令燕且緩豨，而與胡連和？

translations.literal: Why not let Yan for now ease pressure on Xi and make alliance and peace with the Hu?
translations.idiomatic: Why not have Yan hold back against Chen Xi for now and make terms with the Hu?

sentences.zh: 事寬，得長王燕，即有漢急，可以安國。」

translations.literal: If affairs relax, you can long remain king of Yan. Then if Han has urgent pressure, you can secure your state.
translations.idiomatic: With tensions eased, Yan can remain secure longer, and if Han later turns urgent, your state will be safer.

sentences.zh: 勝以為然，乃私令匈奴兵擊燕。

translations.literal: Sheng took this as right, and privately ordered Xiongnu troops to attack Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Sheng accepted this and privately arranged for Xiongnu forces to strike Yan.

sentences.zh: 綰疑勝與胡反，上書請族勝。

translations.literal: Wan suspected Sheng had rebelled with the Hu and submitted memorial requesting execution of Sheng's clan.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Wan suspected Zhang Sheng had gone over to the Hu and petitioned to exterminate his clan.

sentences.zh: 勝還報，具道所以為者。

translations.literal: Sheng returned and reported, fully explaining the reasons for what he had done.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhang Sheng returned, he explained in full why he had acted that way.

sentences.zh: 綰寤，乃詐論他人，以脫勝家屬，使得為匈奴間。

translations.literal: Wan awakened, then falsely prosecuted another person to free Sheng's family, letting Sheng serve as Han's spy among the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Wan realized the logic and staged another prosecution to spare Zhang Sheng's family, allowing Zhang Sheng to continue as an intelligence asset among the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 而陰使范齊之豨所，欲令久連兵毋決。

translations.literal: And he secretly sent Fan Qi to Xi's place, wishing to make warfare continue long and not be decided.
translations.idiomatic: He also secretly sent Fan Qi to Chen Xi, hoping to prolong the conflict without resolution.

sentences.zh: 漢既斬豨，其裨將降，言燕王綰使范齊通計謀豨所。

translations.literal: After Han had beheaded Xi, Xi's deputy surrendered, saying King Wan of Yan had sent Fan Qi to communicate plans with Xi.
translations.idiomatic: After Han executed Chen Xi, a surrendered deputy testified that Lu Wan had sent Fan Qi to coordinate with Chen Xi.

sentences.zh: 上使使召綰，綰稱病。

translations.literal: The emperor sent envoy to summon Wan, and Wan claimed illness.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor summoned Lu Wan, but Lu Wan claimed illness.

sentences.zh: 又使辟陽侯審食其、御史大夫趙堯往迎綰，因驗問其左右。

translations.literal: He again sent Marquis Piyang Shen Yiqi and Censor-in-Chief Zhao Yao to receive Wan and by the way verify and question his attendants.
translations.idiomatic: He then sent Marquis Piyang Shen Yiqi and Censor Zhao Yao to escort Lu Wan and quietly question his entourage.

sentences.zh: 綰愈恐，閟匿，謂其幸臣曰：「非劉氏而王者，獨我與長沙耳。

translations.literal: Wan became more afraid, hid away, and told his favored retainers: among non-Liu men made kings, only I and Changsha remain.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Wan grew more frightened and hid himself, telling close retainers: among non-Liu kings, only I and Changsha remain.

sentences.zh: 往年漢族淮陰，誅彭越，皆呂后計。

translations.literal: In past years Han exterminated Huaiyin and executed Peng Yue; all were plans of Empress Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Han Xin and Peng Yue were killed under Empress Lü's plotting.

sentences.zh: 今上病，屬任呂后。

translations.literal: Now the emperor is ill and entrusts authority to Empress Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Now the emperor is sick and has entrusted affairs to Empress Lü.

sentences.zh: 呂后婦人，專欲以事誅異姓王者及大功臣。」

translations.literal: Empress Lu is a woman who focuses only on using pretexts to execute non-Liu kings and great merit ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lü, being what she is, aims to use charges to eliminate non-Liu kings and powerful merit officials.

sentences.zh: 乃稱病不行。

translations.literal: So he claimed illness and did not go.
translations.idiomatic: So he claimed illness and refused to go.

sentences.zh: 其左右皆亡匿。

translations.literal: His attendants all fled and hid.
translations.idiomatic: His attendants scattered and went into hiding.

sentences.zh: 語頗泄，辟陽侯聞之，歸具報，上益怒。

translations.literal: The talk leaked somewhat. Marquis Piyang heard it, returned and fully reported, and the emperor became even angrier.
translations.idiomatic: The remarks leaked. Marquis Piyang heard them and reported everything, further enraging the emperor.

sentences.zh: 又得匈奴降者，言張勝亡在匈奴，為燕使。

translations.literal: Also, they got a surrendered Xiongnu who said Zhang Sheng had fled into Xiongnu and was acting as envoy for Yan.
translations.idiomatic: A surrendered Xiongnu also testified that Zhang Sheng was in Xiongnu as an agent for Yan.

sentences.zh: 於是上曰：「綰果反矣！」

translations.literal: Then the emperor said: Wan has indeed rebelled!
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then said, Lu Wan has truly rebelled.

sentences.zh: 使樊噲擊綰。
