translations.literal: He sent Fan Kuai to attack Wan.
translations.idiomatic: He sent Fan Kuai to attack Lu Wan.

sentences.zh: 綰悉將其宮人家屬，騎數千，居長城下候伺，幸上病瘉，自入謝。

translations.literal: Wan took all his palace people and family dependents, several thousand cavalry, and stayed below the Great Wall to wait and watch, hoping the emperor's illness would recover so he could enter and apologize in person.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Wan gathered his household and several thousand cavalry and camped near the Great Wall, hoping the emperor would recover so he could submit in person.

sentences.zh: 高祖崩，綰遂將其眾亡入匈奴，匈奴以為東胡盧王。

translations.literal: When Gaozu died, Wan then led his followers and fled into Xiongnu. Xiongnu made him king of Donghu Lu.
translations.idiomatic: When Gaozu died, Lu Wan led his followers into Xiongnu territory, where he was made king of Donghu Lu.

sentences.zh: 為蠻夷所侵奪，常思復歸。

translations.literal: Being invaded and seized by tribal peoples, he often thought of returning.
translations.idiomatic: Harried by steppe tribes, he often longed to return.

sentences.zh: 居歲餘，死胡中。

translations.literal: After living over a year, he died among the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: After a little over a year, he died in Xiongnu lands.

sentences.zh: 高后時，綰妻與其子亡降，會高后病，不能見，舍燕邸，為欲置酒見之。

translations.literal: In Empress Gao's time, Wan's wife and son fled and surrendered. It happened that Empress Gao was ill and could not see them. They lodged at the Yan residence, with plans to hold wine and receive them.
translations.idiomatic: During Empress Lü's rule, Lu Wan's wife and son fled and surrendered, but because the empress was ill she could not receive them, so they were lodged at the Yan residence pending audience.

sentences.zh: 高后竟崩，綰妻亦病死。

translations.literal: Empress Gao soon died, and Wan's wife also died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lü soon died, and Lu Wan's wife also died of illness.

sentences.zh: 孝景帝時，綰孫它人以東胡王降，封為惡谷侯。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing's time, Wan's grandson Taren surrendered as king of Donghu and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Egu.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Jing, Lu Wan's grandson Taren surrendered from his position as Donghu king and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Egu.

sentences.zh: 傳至曾孫，有罪，國除。

translations.literal: The line passed to a great-grandson. He committed offense and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The title passed to a great-grandson, who was punished for a crime and lost the fief.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：昔高祖定天下，功臣異姓而王者八國。

translations.literal: The appraisal says: formerly when Gaozu settled all under Heaven, among meritorious ministers of different surnames there were eight kingdoms made kings.
translations.idiomatic: Appraisal: when Gaozu unified the realm, he enfeoffed eight non-Liu kingdoms among his merit officials.

sentences.zh: 張耳、吳芮、彭越、黥布、臧荼、盧綰與兩韓信，皆徼一時之權變，以詐力成功，咸得裂土，南面稱孤。

translations.literal: Zhang Er, Wu Rui, Peng Yue, Qing Bu, Zang Tu, Lu Wan, and the two Han Xins all seized temporary shifts of power, succeeded through cunning and force, and all gained divided lands, facing south and calling themselves solitary rulers.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er, Wu Rui, Peng Yue, Ying Bu, Zang Tu, Lu Wan, and the two Han Xins all rose through opportunistic power shifts and force, gaining territories and ruling as independent kings.

sentences.zh: 見疑強大，懷不自安，事窮勢迫，卒謀叛逆，終於滅亡。

translations.literal: Once suspected for being strong, they were inwardly uneasy. Affairs reached dead ends and pressure forced them. In the end they plotted rebellion and ended in destruction.
translations.idiomatic: Once their strength aroused suspicion, they became insecure. Pressed into corners, they turned to rebellion and met destruction.

sentences.zh: 張耳以智全，至子亦失國。

translations.literal: Zhang Er preserved himself by wisdom, yet by his son also lost the state.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er saved himself through prudence, yet his line still lost the kingdom in his son's generation.

sentences.zh: 唯吳芮之起，不失正道，故能傳號五世，以無嗣絕，慶流支庶。

translations.literal: Only in Wu Rui's rise did he not lose the proper way, therefore he could pass the title for five generations, and though direct heirs ended, blessings flowed to collateral branches.
translations.idiomatic: Only Wu Rui rose without abandoning proper conduct; thus his title lasted five generations, and even after the main line ended, fortune continued in collateral branches.

sentences.zh: 有以矣夫，著于甲令而稱忠也！

translations.literal: There was indeed reason for it, as recorded in formal statutes and praised as loyalty.
translations.idiomatic: There is good reason for this outcome, as recorded in legal registers where he is praised for loyalty.
